extractedFieldValues.current_value: James Helms, a 90-year-old male, is being referred for home health care following an encounter for change or removal of a non-surgical wound dressing, with a chronic ulcer on another part of the left foot with the fat layer exposed; he recently underwent a revascularization procedure due to significant Peripheral Artery Disease and has a left second toe ulceration at risk for infection requiring skilled wound care, with additional conditions including cellulitis of the left foot and a blister on the left foot, and following vascular surgery he continues to experience pain and significant swelling in his lower extremities, indicating compromised circulation and the need for ongoing monitoring and skilled nursing care.
source_spans: Primary Reason for Home Health/Medical Necessity (POC Element):

James Helms, a 90-year-old male, is being referred for home health care following an encounter for change or removal of a non-surgical wound dressing, with a chronic ulcer on another part of the left foot with the fat layer exposed; he recently underwent a revascularization procedure due to significant Peripheral Artery Disease and has a left second toe ulceration at risk for infection requiring skilled wound care, with additional conditions including cellulitis of the left foot and a blister on the left foot, and following vascular surgery he continues to experience pain and significant swelling in his lower extremities, indicating compromised circulation and the need for ongoing monitoring and skilled nursing care.
